title: Grounding
description: The live Boutique path grounds recommendations in Aurora data: product_catalog for products, inventory/quantity fields for availability, return policy tables for support, and pgvector/FTS for retrieval.
codeSnippet:
# Ground a recommendation in catalog rows
SELECT product_id, name, brand, price, quantity
FROM product_catalog
WHERE quantity > 0
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
LIMIT 5;

title: Memory
description: Short-term memory keeps recent turns scoped by session namespace. Long-term preference facts are tied to a persona/customer and surfaced as context for the specialist, with an in-memory fallback when AgentCore Memory is not provisioned.
codeSnippet:
# STM — namespace-scoped session history
await memory.append_session_turn(namespace, turn)
history = await memory.get_session_history(namespace)

# Persistent preferences
prefs = await memory.get_user_preferences(user_id)

title: Skills
description: Three persona skills — the-packing-list, the-gift-table, and the-makers-shelf — are loaded by the SkillRouter when a turn needs persona-specific handling. They shape voice and handling, not the database contract.
codeSnippet:
# SkillRouter may load one persona skill per turn
skills = route_skills(query, persona)
prompt += inject_loaded_skills(skills)

title: Routing & State
description: The production Boutique path uses deterministic routing in services/chat.py: triage, intent classification, optional skill routing, then one owning specialist/tool path. The Agents-as-Tools orchestrator is a separate teaching pattern, not the default storefront runtime.
codeSnippet:
# Boutique default path
intent = classify_intent(query)
skills = route_skills(query, persona)
return await run_dispatcher_pattern(intent, skills, query)

title: Runtime Envelope
role: Execution environment and workshop runtime pattern
description: Runtime is the managed envelope around execution: model calls, memory, Gateway, and observability. In this codebase, the default Boutique flow is implemented in the app service layer; AgentCore Runtime concepts are surfaced as workshop infrastructure and deployment patterns.
codeSnippet:
# App-layer runtime shape
request → triage → dispatcher → specialist/tool → SSE telemetry

# Managed runtime is introduced as the deployment envelope
# around the same agent/tool contracts.

title: Evaluations
description: Evaluation cards are the owned quality layer: latency, relevance, grounding, and scenario checks for the three workshop capabilities. They are not the request path; they are how the team decides whether a path is worth shipping.
codeSnippet:
# Evaluation scorecard shape
scorecard = {
    'capability': 'agentic (Haiku → filter → vector → rerank)',
    'recall_at_5': 0.96,
    'latency_p50_ms': 2300,
    'grounded': true
}
